LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER TRANSPORT WORKERS UNION LOCAL 200 HEARING ON PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION CONTINUED FROM MAY-22-2008 TO LAW AND MOTION AT JUN-18-2008, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302, PER STIPULATION & ORDER OF 05/14/08.
REPLY MEMORANDUM OF P/A IN SUPPORT OF PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. FILED BY PETITIONER TRANSPORT WORKERS UNION LOCAL 200

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER TRANSPORT WORKERS UNION LOCAL 200'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS DENIED. THE MOU, RELIED ON BY PETITIONER, WAS IN EFFECT DURING 2006-2007 FISCAL YEAR. PETITIONER HAS STATED UNEQUIVOCALLY THAT "THERE WAS NO VIOLATION OF THE AGREEMENT FOR FISCAL YEAR 2006-2007". IT ALSO IS UNDISPUTED THAT THE PARTIES ENTERED INTO A SUBSEQUENT MOU GOVERNING THE RIGHTS AND OBLIGATIONS OF THE PARTIES AFTER THE EXPIRATION TO THE 2006-2007 AGREEMENT. AS THE JUNE 19, 2007 GRIEVANCE MAKES CLEAR, PETITIONER SEEKS TO ARBITRATE A BREACH THAT OCCURS AFTER THE 2006-2007 CONTRACT HAS ENDED BUT IS NOT SEEKING TO ARBITRATE THE ISSUE UNDER THE SUBSEQUENT AGREEMENT; RATHER PETITIONER SEEKS TO ARBITRATE THE DISPUTE BASED ON AN AGREEMENT THAT PETITIONER ADMITS HAS NOT BEEN VIOLATED. IT FOLLOWS, THEREFORE, THAT SINCE THE AGREEMENT RELIED UPON BY PETITIONER ADMITTEDLY HAS NOT BEEN BREACHED, THERE IS NO BASIS TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. IT WOULD SIMPLY BE AN IDLE ACT. THERE ALSO IS A MATERIAL ISSUE AS TO PETITIONER'S WAIVER OF THIS CLAIM GIVEN THE APRIL 12, 2007 GRIEVANCE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER TRANSPORT WORKERS UNION LOCAL 200'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS CONTINUED FROM JUN-18-2008 TO JUL-16-2008 ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION. JUDGE: PAUL H. ALVARADO, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER TRANSPORT WORKERS UNION LOCAL 200'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE MATTER IS CONTINUED TO AUGUST 12, 2008 FOR FURTHER HEARING. PARTIES MAY FILE SIMULTANEOUS BRIEFS (NOT TO EXCEED 7 PAGES) BY AUGUST 6, 2008 ADDRESSING THE FOLLOWING ISSUES: 1- SET OUT ANALYSIS AS TO WHY THE COURT SHOULD CONCLUDE EITHER THAT THE UNION MAY OR MAY NOT RAISE THE SAME GRIEVANCE; FOCUS ON THE ISSUE TO BE ARBITRATED AND WHY OR WHY NOT THE JUNE 18 LETTER AND THE RELEVANCE OF THE JUNE 18 LETTER TO THAT ISSUE AS WELL AS RELEVANCE OR PERSUASIVENESS OF THE DETERMINATION BY THE INTEREST ARBITRATION PROCEEDINGS; 2- BOTH SIDES TO ADDRESS (BETWEEN THE PLATT CASE AND THE NAPA CASE) WHICH IS MORE PERSUASIVE AND WHY; AND 3- CITE OTHER CASES (NO MORE THAN 3) THAT THE PARTIES BELIEVE SUPPORT THEIR ARGUMENT. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration0
Off Calendar Aug-12-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Scott DeNardo and Benjamin Lunch of Neyhart Anderson Flynn & Grossboll (677.9440 x196) for petitioner and moving party
Jill Figg of the Office of the City Attorney (554.3971) for respondent and opposing party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: PETITIONER TRANSPORT WORKERS UNION LOCAL 200'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION is denied. The MOU, relied on by Petitioner, was in effect during 2006-2007 fiscal year. Petitioner has stated unequivocally that "there was no violation of the agreement for fiscal year 2006-2007". It also is undisputed that the parties entered into a subsequent MOU governing the rights and obligations of the parties after the expiration to the 2006-2007 agreement. As the June 19, 2007 grievance makes clear, Petitioner seeks to arbitrate a breach that occurs after the 2006-2007 contract has ended but is not seeking to arbitrate the issue under the subsequent agreement; rather Petitioner seeks to arbitrate the dispute based on an agreement that Petitioner admits has not been violated. It follows, therefore, that since the agreement relied upon by Petitioner admittedly has not been breached, there is no basis to compel arbitration. It would simply be an idle act. There also is a material issue as to Petitioner's waiver of this claim given the April 12, 2007 grievance. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

Ntc Of Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration0
Off Calendar Jul-16-2008 Continued To Aug-12-08 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER TRANSPORT WORKERS UNION LOCAL 200'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION

Appearances:
Jill Figg of the Office of the City Attorney (554-3971) for respondent and opposing party
Scott DeNardo and Benjamin Lunch of Neyhart Anderson Flynn & Grosboll (677-9440) for petitioner and moving party

Argued and the Court modified its ruling as follows: the matter is continued to August 12, 2008 for further hearing. Parties may file simultaneous briefs (not to exceed 7 pages) by August 6, 2008 addressing the following issues: 1- set out analysis as to why the Court should conclude either that the union may or may not raise the same grievance; focus on the issue to be arbitrated and why or why not the June 18 letter and the relevance of the June 18 letter to that issue as well as relevance or persuasiveness of the determination by the interest arbitration proceedings; 2- both sides to address (between the Platt case and the Napa case) which is more persuasive and why; and 3- cite other cases (no more than 3) that the parties believe support their argument. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
